content.full_text:
S4220, at pag

6220?

October 22, 1976

96-18-147

Mr. Jack Heinrich, Jr.
Kaiser Industries
300 Lakeside Drive
Oakland, California  94666

Dear Mr. Heinrich:

This letter will serve to confirm the advice given to you on
October 18, concerning the filing schedule to be followed by
Kaiser Industries in connection with the filing of campaign
statements for the 1976 general election.

You have stated that although a contribution to a ballot measure
was contemplated prior to the closing date of the required filing
for ballot measures, none was made.  Thus, Kaiser had no obligation
to file campaign statements with respect to ballot measures as of
the closing date of the first pre-election statement.  A contribution was, however, made to a candidate during that period which
triggered an obligation to file on the candidate's schedule.
Kaiser Industries was required, therefore, to file a campaign
report on the candidate's schedule.  Having filed a single campaign
statement on the candidate's schedule, the company may not alter
its filing dates to follow the ballot measure schedule.

Section 84204, which allows a committee the option to file on
either schedule, applies only if at the time of the first filing
date for the general election you had an obligation to file in
connection with both candidates and ballot measures.

Very truly yours,


REGINA SICILIANO-KUTCHINS
Technical Assistance Program
Coordinator


RSK:mak
bcc:  Jeanne

KAISER
INDUSTRIES CORPORATION

KAISER CENTER · 300 LAKESIDE DRIVE · OAKLAND, CALIFORNIA 94606 · TELEPHONE 415/271-2211

EXECUTIVE OFFICES

October 7, 1976

Ms. Nan Hambleton
Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance Division
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Major Donor Campaign Statements
    of Kaiser Industries Corporation

Dear Ms. Hambleton:

Confirming our recent telephone conversation, Kaiser Industries requests a letter of
technical advice from the FPPC with regard to its options under Government Code
Section 84204. Kaiser Industries and certain affiliates have now made contributions
to both candidates and ballot measures for the forthcoming election. It therefore
claims that it may file its campaign statements under either the candidate or ballot
measure filing schedule. (Sec. 84204.) A contribution to a ballot measure was
planned to be made prior to the deadline for ballot measure filing. However, on
September 24 the mailing of the contribution was inadvertently delayed until September 27, which was after the deadline for filing the first campaign statement. Your
initial reaction was that this fact required us to follow the candidate filing schedule
for future statements in this election. We do not find such a limitation in Section
84204, as that section plainly gives us a choice because we are "required to file
campaign statements with respect to both candidates and measures at the same
election." Since we have now made a contribution to a ballot measure and will
incur reportable expenses communicating our position on this measure, we prefer
to follow the ballot measure filing schedule. Such a filing schedule would be
consistent with the schedule we followed in the primary election.

We would appreciate it if you would review this matter further and send me written
technical advice. Thanks for your help.

Very truly yours,

KAISER INDUSTRIES CORPORATION

[signature]
Jack Heinrich, Jr., Coordinator
Corporate Compliance Reporting

sections.question: Kaiser Industries requests a letter of technical advice from the FPPC with regard to its options under Government Code Section 84204. Kaiser Industries and certain affiliates have now made contributions to both candidates and ballot measures for the forthcoming election. It therefore claims that it may file its campaign statements under either the candidate or ballot measure filing schedule. A contribution to a ballot measure was planned to be made prior to the deadline for ballot measure filing. However, on September 24 the mailing of the contribution was inadvertently delayed until September 27, which was after the deadline for filing the first campaign statement. The question is whether this delay requires following the candidate filing schedule for future statements in this election, or whether Section 84204 allows choice of filing schedule because contributions were made to both candidates and measures.
sections.conclusion: You have stated that although a contribution to a ballot measure was contemplated prior to the closing date of the required filing for ballot measures, none was made. Thus, Kaiser had no obligation to file campaign statements with respect to ballot measures as of the closing date of the first pre-election statement. A contribution was, however, made to a candidate during that period which triggered an obligation to file on the candidate's schedule. Kaiser Industries was required, therefore, to file a campaign report on the candidate's schedule. Having filed a single campaign statement on the candidate's schedule, the company may not alter its filing dates to follow the ballot measure schedule. Section 84204, which allows a committee the option to file on either schedule, applies only if at the time of the first filing date for the general election you had an obligation to file in connection with both candidates and ballot measures.
sections.question_synthetic: Can Kaiser Industries choose between candidate and ballot measure filing schedules under Section 84204 when a ballot measure contribution was delayed past the initial filing deadline, despite having made contributions to both candidates and ballot measures?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Kaiser Industries must follow the candidate filing schedule because it made a contribution to a candidate before the first pre-election filing deadline, while its ballot measure contribution was delayed past that deadline. Section 84204's optional filing schedule choice applies only when obligations to file exist for both candidates and ballot measures at the time of the first filing date, which did not occur here.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of two letters: the incoming request dated October 7, 1976 from Kaiser Industries to FPPC, followed by FPPC's response letter dated October 22, 1976. The response clearly identifies the question and provides definitive conclusion on filing schedule requirements under Section 84204.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Kaiser Industries requests a letter of technical advice from the FPPC with regard to its options under Government Code Section 84204. Kaiser Industries and certain affiliates have now made contributions to both candidates and ballot measures for the forthcoming election. It therefore claims that it may file its campaign statements under either the candidate or ballot measure filing schedule. A contribution to a ballot measure was planned to be made prior to the deadline for ballot measure filing. However, on September 24 the mailing of the contribution was inadvertently delayed until September 27, which was after the deadline for filing the first campaign statement. The question is whether this delay requires following the candidate filing schedule for future statements in this election, or whether Section 84204 allows choice of filing schedule because contributions were made to both candidates and measures.

CONCLUSION: You have stated that although a contribution to a ballot measure was contemplated prior to the closing date of the required filing for ballot measures, none was made. Thus, Kaiser had no obligation to file campaign statements with respect to ballot measures as of the closing date of the first pre-election statement. A contribution was, however, made to a candidate during that period which triggered an obligation to file on the candidate's schedule. Kaiser Industries was required, therefore, to file a campaign report on the candidate's schedule. Having filed a single campaign statement on the candidate's schedule, the company may not alter its filing dates to follow the ballot measure schedule. Section 84204, which allows a committee the option to file on either schedule, applies only if at the time of the first filing date for the general election you had an obligation to file in connection with both candidates and ballot measures.

Very truly yours, REGINA SICILIANO-KUTCHINS Technical Assistance Program Coordinator RSK:mak bcc: Jeanne October 7, 1976 Ms. Nan Hambleton Fair Political Practices Commission Technical Assistance Division P.O. embedding.summary: FPPC advises Kaiser Industries that it must use the candidate filing schedule for the 1976 general election because its ballot measure contribution was delayed past the initial filing deadline, and Section 84204 only permits schedule choice when filing obligations exist for both candidates and measures at the first filing date.
